hunt.author: Elastic
hunt.name: Unusual Library Load via Python
hunt.description: Detects when a library is loaded from a user's home directory by a Python process and the loaded file is not a typical shared object (.so) or dynamic library (.dylib). This may indicate side-loading of malicious or non-standard files in script-based execution environments.
notes: Loading libraries from /Users is rare and may suggest untrusted or attacker-deployed components.
This hunt helps uncover suspicious Python-driven library loads that bypass traditional extension-based detection.
Consider tuning to exclude known development or research environments that store legitimate libraries in home directories.
query:
library where event.action == "load" and
  dll.path like "/Users/*" and
  process.name like~ "python*" and
  not dll.name : ("*.so", "*.dylib")

library where event.action == "load" and
  dll.path like "/Users/*" and
  process.name like~ "python*" and
  not dll.name : ("*.so", "*.dylib")
rule.description: Detects when a library is loaded from a user's home directory by a Python process and the loaded file is not a typical shared object (.so) or dynamic library (.dylib). This may indicate side-loading of malicious or non-standard files in script-based execution environments.
